reasoning: According to the search summary, TSLA's year-to-date low in 2025 was $222 in March, and as of mid-September 2025, it was trading near $396. To drop below $200 before the end of 2025 (a period of roughly two months left from the October 26 cutoff date), the stock would need to plummet approximately 50%. While TSLA is highly volatile (e.g. it dropped from $479 to $222 between Dec 2024 and Mar 2025), a 50% drop in just over two months is a tail-risk event. Assuming an implied volatility of 80%, an options-based (Black-Scholes) approximation yields a ~2.5% chance of the stock halving over a two-month timeframe. Considering the absence of any mentions of a new autumn collapse in the search summary, I predict a very low likelihood that TSLA falls below $200 before the end of 2025.
